title: Change rmi --prune to not accept an imageID
body:
You cannot enter image when using the prune option.

After change:
```
➜  buildah git:(rmi-prune-fix) ✗ sudo ./buildah rmi -p 165
when using the --prune switch, you may not pass any images names or IDs
```

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body:
@q384566678 Thanks for the PR.  The code looks fine, but I'm wondering if you had run into an issue pruning just one image?  I'm not seeing the need to not allow an image.  I know that's not Docker compatible, but I like the ability as long as it's working.

Test issues appear to not be related.  I think it's due to the move to containers from projectatomic.

@TomSweeneyRedHat What does it mean to --prune an image?
`podman rmi --prune fedora`
How is this different then
`podman rmi fedora`

```
➜  buildah git:(rmi-prune-fix) ✗ sudo buildah images -a

16508e5c265d         docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest                          Aug 23, 2018 01:28     86.6 MB
e1ddd7948a1c         docker.io/library/busybox:latest                         Aug 1, 2018 06:20      1.38 MB
71c43202b8ac         docker.io/library/nginx:latest                           Aug 29, 2018 23:29     113 MB

18f01f6f77ef         registry.fedoraproject.org/f27/httpd:latest              Jul 18, 2018 04:08     426 MB

f422e539dbee         <none>                                                   Sep 19, 2018 10:15     1.38 MB
➜  buildah git:(rmi-prune-fix) ✗ sudo buildah rmi -p 1650
[sudo] password for zhouhao: 
51aab452fba9ae07f1c948d593dc27bb2c4f108e7ad31cef51ee749cbece21e3
ddea22402ca846031374f4d1fd8c2b90b5f9b21a8261cecd7ad093e26a8a4a0a
81dd0e46f76f85b450ed130faf20bc389936ee3aa5ba16e43770f58acdaa3076
892b3b03587e1864aac12c34f3ce69150d090f6d73aea73a396ba94e836e657f
```

Before the modification, only the `prune` option will be run, and the command to `rmi 1650` cannot be executed, so I think it needs to be modified and an error message will appear.
```
➜  buildah git:(rmi-prune-fix) ✗ sudo buildah images -a  
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
16508e5c265d         docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest                          Aug 23, 2018 01:28     86.6 MB
e1ddd7948a1c         docker.io/library/busybox:latest                         Aug 1, 2018 06:20      1.38 MB
71c43202b8ac         docker.io/library/nginx:latest                           Aug 29, 2018 23:29     113 MB
aa225d22f77c         localhost/foo:latest                                     Aug 30, 2018 16:50     1.38 MB
18f01f6f77ef         registry.fedoraproject.org/f27/httpd:latest              Jul 18, 2018 04:08     426 MB
➜  buildah git:(rmi-prune-fix) ✗ sudo buildah rmi -p 1650
➜  buildah git:(rmi-prune-fix) ✗ sudo buildah images -a  
IMAGE ID             IMAGE NAME                                               CREATED AT             SIZE
16508e5c265d         docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest                          Aug 23, 2018 01:28     86.6 MB
e1ddd7948a1c         docker.io/library/busybox:latest                         Aug 1, 2018 06:20      1.38 MB
71c43202b8ac         docker.io/library/nginx:latest                           Aug 29, 2018 23:29     113 MB
aa225d22f77c         localhost/foo:latest                                     Aug 30, 2018 16:50     1.38 MB
18f01f6f77ef         registry.fedoraproject.org/f27/httpd:latest              Jul 18, 2018 04:08     426 MB
```

@rhatdan hmm, never saw your reply in e-mail.  Anywho, when you add the -prune parameter, it removes images if and only if they do not have a tag and don't have a child image pointing to them.  Kind of a quick way to remove images that aren't in use and keep the ones you care about.

@q384566678 I think what you're saying is that image 1650 was not deleted if you used  the command 'buildah rmi -p 1650'.  As it has a tag, it really shouldn't be removed in this situation.  The prune parameter should basically remove the `'<none>'` images.  I think if we looked at your first example:

```
892b3b03587e         <none>                                                   Sep 19, 2018 10:14     1.38 MB
```
`buildah rmi -p 892b3b03587e` 

with your change would fail when it should succeed.  I will admit though that having an image id as part of the `buildah rmi -p` command is unusual, but I don't think we should prevent it.  For the most part folks are going to use the command to clean up the <nonne> images.

So Tom you say that 
```
buildah rmi -p 892b3b03587e
```
Should Prune and Remove 892b3b03587e

But currently the command only prunes.  I am fine with either solution.  Since I don't think the user case of -p IMAGEID will be common.

The `buildah rmi -p 892b3b03587e` worked for me once I removed the containers that were dependent on it.   But thinking about all of it, I'd be OK with this change.  I don't think many if any people would use the command with an image id any way.  If they wanted to remove with the id, they can just do the non-prune route.

@q384566678 Thanks for working through this with us.  Could you change this commit's title to something like:

"Change rmi --prune to not accept an imageID"

Also the rmi man page will need tweaking too.
@rh-atomic-bot r+

Unrelated test failure.  Will try again.  @q384566678 After this merges I'll put together a PR to handle the man page change.  I also just noted a very small grammar problem in this code, I'll touch that up too.
@rh-atomic-bot r+
bot, retest this please
@rh-atomic-bot r+
@jlebon Since we moved from projectatomic/buildah to containers/buildah.  rh-atomic-bot has not been working.  Any ideas?

@jlebon: :key: Insufficient privileges: Collaborator required
Ahh OK, got `EPERM`. Should work fine now though.
